title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 14, 2026
headline: Bolivia's June 9 state-of-exception law — predicted in the June 13 briefing as the threshold-crossing trigger — is confirmed today, driving the country from 12.8 to a proposed 6.3 as military deployment against protesters is authorized, the 60-day cap and deployment restrictions repealed, and the ACC dimension reaches floor; nineteen confirmations include Anthropic at 58.8 (Fable/Mythos suspension follow-through), US at 16.2 (ICE death-reporting memo + Medicaid frailty narrowing), and Oracle at 14.7 (June 15 deadline with no new evidence beyond the sign-or-forfeit structure already priced).
summary: Tonight's cycle produced one change proposal and nineteen confirmations across 20 assessed entities. Bolivia is the lead signal: President Paz signed the Law Regulating States of Exception on June 9, 2026, authorizing military deployment against anti-government protesters, eliminating the prior 60-day cap on emergency duration, removing restrictions on military deployment during demonstrations, and enabling up-to-8-hour detentions. The Ombudsman documented 10 dead, 37 injured, and 365 arrests by June 2, with six deaths attributed to blocked medical access. This is the event the June 13 digest explicitly predicted as the ACC-dimension floor trigger; the proposal moves Bolivia from 12.8 to 6.3 (delta -6.5, high confidence, within-Critical deepening). The ACC dimension moves to floor (1.0), with INT also reaching 1.0 on Paz's evidence-free narco-terrorism framing. Nineteen other entities confirm: Anthropic holds at 58.8 following its June 13 compliance with the Commerce Department export-control order disabling Fable 5 and Mythos 5, with the published 59.1 confirmed net within noise; the United States holds at 16.2 (ICE eliminated post-release death reporting via June 4 memo; Medicaid frailty exemption narrowed June 3) with a -1.3 sub-threshold delta below the 5-point threshold; Oracle holds at 14.7 with zero delta (June 15 layoff-deadline review surfaced no new evidence beyond the coercive sign-or-forfeit structure already priced on June 12); Israel and Russia reinforce at 0.0; Nigeria, Mali, Niger, Chad, and Burkina Faso all confirm via freshness ruling; five Fortune 500 rotation entities confirm at the default Developing profile.

pipeline.errorNote: OpenAI 42-state AG probe (June 12-13) referenced in task brief; OpenAI was not in scan scope; Anthropic assessed as the AI-labs priority entity instead.

topSignals.title: Bolivia 12.8 → Proposed 6.3 (Δ -6.5): June 9 State-of-Exception Law Confirms Predicted ACC-Floor Trigger
topSignals.description: President Paz signed the Law Regulating States of Exception on June 9, 2026 — the threshold-crossing event the June 13 briefing identified as the clearest single trigger for Bolivia's ACC dimension reaching floor. The law authorizes military deployment against anti-government protesters and enables suspension of constitutional rights. Prior safeguards were repealed: the 60-day cap on emergency duration was eliminated and restrictions on military deployment during demonstrations were removed; the law permits up-to-8-hour detentions for non-compliance. The national Ombudsman documented 10 dead, 37 injured, and 365 arrests by June 2; six deaths are attributed to blocked access to timely medical care. President Paz justified the law with evidence-free narco-terrorism claims, an accountability-deflection pattern that drives the INT dimension to floor alongside ACC. Bolivia's published 12.8 — itself the result of the June 12 within-Critical deepening proposal now applied — predates the enactment of this law. The proposal moves Bolivia to 6.3 (delta -6.5, high confidence): ACC to 1.0 (floor), INT to 1.0, EMP to 1.3, EQU to 1.2, BND to 1.2, ACT to 1.3, AWR to 1.5, SYS to 1.5. No band change (Critical remains); the movement is a within-Critical deepening toward the floor. Bolivia has now recorded three consecutive downward assessment cycles in eight days: June 9 band crossing (28.4 → 18.4, Developing → Critical), June 12 within-Critical deepening (18.4 → 12.8, applied), and tonight's proposal (12.8 → 6.3 proposed). The predicted-to-realized continuity across the June 13 digest and tonight's assessment is the defining analytical feature of this cycle.

evidence.quote: He stated protest organizers' "days are numbered" and claimed security risks from "narco-terrorism," though journalist Joseph Bouchard noted Paz "did not provide any evidence" connecting protests to drug trafficking.
evidence.claim: President Paz signed emergency-powers legislation on June 9, 2026 and justified it with evidence-free narco-terrorism claims, driving the INT dimension to floor.

evidence.quote: the military to deploy to clear about 90 blockades and other protests
evidence.claim: The June 9, 2026 law authorizes military deployment against protesters and can suspend constitutional rights.

evidence.quote: Opposition lawmakers and human-rights advocates have questioned whether military personnel are appropriately trained to manage demonstrations without escalating them further.
evidence.claim: Bolivia's legislature passed (June 8) and President Paz signed (June 9) a law authorizing armed-forces deployment against protesters, drawing cross-party human-rights concern.

evidence.quote: at least nine people have died as a direct result of the blockades
evidence.claim: Multiple protest-related deaths occurred during the 2026 unrest, including six from failure to receive timely medical attention.

topSignals.title: Anthropic 59.1 Confirmed at 58.8 (Δ -0.3): Fable/Mythos Suspension Follow-Through Within Noise of Published Score
topSignals.description: Anthropic disabled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 13, 2026 under a Commerce Department export-control order. Anthropic complied same-day, issued a public apology, and publicly contested the finding's proportionality — preserving the transparent-compliance posture that the June 13 digest established as net-neutral to mildly positive on the Accountability dimension. Tonight's assessment places the composite at 58.8, a delta of -0.3 against the published 59.1, well within the 5-point threshold. The published score is confirmed. This is the second consecutive cycle in which Anthropic appears as the priority AI-labs assessment entity; the Fable/Mythos event remains the first government-mandated AI model suspension documented across the benchmark's AI-labs index.

topSignals.title: United States 17.5 Confirmed at 16.2 (Δ -1.3): ICE Death-Reporting Elimination + Medicaid Frailty Narrowing Sub-Threshold
topSignals.description: Two new US federal actions compound the already-Critical published score: ICE eliminated post-release death reporting via a June 4 memo, removing independent oversight of in-custody deaths; and the Medicaid frailty exemption was narrowed on June 3, reducing access protections for vulnerable populations. The research composite lands at 16.2, a delta of -1.3 against the published 17.5 — below the 5-point threshold. No proposal generated. The sub-threshold downward drift continues the pattern documented across June 12 (-5.6 proposed and applied) and June 13 (-2.8 sub-threshold): the US's published Critical score is aging against continued deterioration in the ACT and EMP dimensions, but the priced-continuation mechanism holds absent a single 5-point qualifying event.

topSignals.title: Oracle 14.7 Confirmed (Δ 0.0): June 15 Deadline Review Surfaces No New Evidence Beyond Priced Sign-or-Forfeit Structure
topSignals.description: The June 15 layoff-deadline review for Oracle — the primary forward trigger from the June 12 band-crossing proposal — produced a clean confirmation. No new evidence emerged beyond the coercive sign-release-or-forfeit severance structure, WARN pay absorption, and explicit leadership workforce-deprioritization already priced in the applied 14.7 (Critical band). The confirmation at 14.7 with zero delta and high confidence closes the immediate Oracle monitoring window. The next meaningful Oracle signal is any judicial or regulatory adjudication of the WARN-avoidance theory active in Washington state and Missouri.

topSignals.title: Israel and Russia Floor-Confirmed (0.0): Active-Perpetration and Civilian-Strike Evidence Continues to Accumulate
topSignals.description: Israel confirms at 0.0: OCHA (June 5) documents crossing closures and 45 Palestinians killed May 20-June 3; HRW (May 19) documents 856 killed during ceasefire and 50% aid restriction. Russia confirms at 0.0: the June 2 mass strike on Ukraine (UN HRMMU: 22+ civilians killed) plus domestic repression constitute ongoing evidence at the absolute floor. Both entities are already at the harm-flag floor; no further downgrade is methodologically possible.

boundaryWatch.trigger: June 9 state-of-exception law enacted: military deployment authorized, 60-day cap and deployment restrictions repealed, 10 dead, 365 arrests. ACC dimension at floor (1.0). INT dimension at floor on evidence-free narco-terrorism justification.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia at the proposed 6.3 sits 6.3 points above the absolute floor; a documented second harm category at scale or direct military-on-civilian casualty escalation would be the floor-designation conversion trigger. Three consecutive downward cycles in eight days (28.4 → 18.4 → 12.8 → proposed 6.3) constitute the steepest within-band deterioration trajectory in the countries index this assessment cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: ICE post-release death-reporting elimination (June 4 memo) and Medicaid frailty exemption narrowing (June 3) add sub-threshold downward pressure; cumulative sub-threshold delta across four consecutive cycles approaches a full threshold event.
boundaryWatch.note: United States at the assessed 16.2 sits 1.2 points above the absolute floor. The published score (17.5) remains the operative number pending a new proposal. Sub-threshold deterioration has accumulated across four consecutive cycles; the conversion trigger is a single qualifying event in the ICE enforcement, Medicaid coverage, or OBBBA implementation arc.

boundaryWatch.trigger: 34.7M projected food-insecure June-August 2026, with 15,000+ at famine risk in Borno. Phase 5 IPC designation for any individual Nigerian zone would constitute new scorable evidence not currently priced.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 21.9 sits 1.9 points above the Critical boundary. Current score absorbs ongoing-crisis evidence; the Sahel lean season (June-August) is the structural risk period. No in-window country-specific government conduct evidence found this cycle.

emergingRisks.risk: Bolivia continues to accumulate militarization and accountability-suppression evidence at the near-floor of the Critical band; the ACC dimension is now at 1.0.
emergingRisks.detail: The proposed 6.3 sits 6.3 points above the absolute floor. Documented military deployment against civilian protesters, 10 deaths, 365 arrests, and evidence-free official justification collectively constitute the densest ACC-dimension deterioration recorded for Bolivia across any two-week window. A second documented harm category at scale — or direct military-on-civilian casualty escalation beyond the current Ombudsman-documented 10 deaths — would be the floor-designation conversion trigger. The Sahel-style evidence-window problem does not apply here: Bolivia's crisis is recent and well-documented.

emergingRisks.risk: US sub-threshold deterioration is accumulating across the ICE enforcement, Medicaid, and OBBBA arcs toward a potential single-event threshold crossing.
emergingRisks.detail: The ICE post-release death-reporting elimination (June 4) and Medicaid frailty exemption narrowing (June 3) add -1.3 assessed delta to a sub-threshold accumulation that has run for four consecutive cycles. The OBBBA Medicaid work requirements take effect December 31, 2026; CBO projects 10.9-11.8M Americans lose health insurance. When enrollment data materializes, the realized-harm evidence will be scorable as a Tier-1 apply event rather than projected-harm accumulation. The US published 17.5 is already aging below its calibrated level on the sub-threshold evidence record.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Ebola outbreak accelerating into the July natural peak window with sub-20% contact tracing in most affected zones.
emergingRisks.detail: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak continues in Ituri province; response is externally led by WHO, Africa CDC, and US. The July 31 structural review deadline is the monitoring horizon. No Bundibugyo vaccine exists; contact tracing remains below 20% in the most affected zones. Documented state obstruction of the externally-led response would be the conversion trigger from natural-disaster-overlay hold to scored state-conduct event.

emergingRisks.risk: Nigeria and the Sahel lean-season cluster sit at or near the Critical boundary during the structural risk period for Phase 5 IPC famine designations.
emergingRisks.detail: June through August is the Sahel lean season. Nigeria at 21.9 (1.9pt above the Critical boundary) is the most immediately exposed: a Phase 5 IPC famine designation for any individual Nigerian zone would constitute new scorable evidence not currently priced. Mali, Niger, Chad, and Burkina Faso all confirmed via freshness ruling this cycle; in-window Phase 5 evidence in any of these countries would reopen the assessment. The five-country freshness-ruling cluster documented over June 13-14 is a recurring structural feature of the lean-season window.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle's WARN-avoidance litigation arc remains unresolved following the June 15 deadline confirmation; judicial adjudication would reopen the scoring question.
emergingRisks.detail: Oracle confirmed at 14.7 with zero new evidence at the June 15 deadline. The Washington state class action and Missouri investigation on the WARN-avoidance theory are unresolved; these are the only remaining evidence threads that could generate a new scoring event for Oracle without a materially different corporate action. An adverse judicial or regulatory ruling would be a Tier-1 apply trigger distinct from the coercive-severance structure already priced.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Bolivia: Predicted-to-Realized Continuity in the Critical Band
sectorAlerts.headline: Bolivia's three-cycle descent (28.4 → 18.4 → 12.8 → proposed 6.3) over eight days is the steepest within-band deterioration trajectory in the current assessment cycle series
observations: June 9 cycle: band crossing applied (Developing → Critical, -10.0). June 12 cycle: within-Critical deepening applied (-5.6, 18.4 → 12.8). June 14 cycle: within-Critical deepening proposed (-6.5, 12.8 → 6.3). Three proposals in five assessments, all downward.

The June 13 digest explicitly identified the state-of-exception law as the threshold-crossing trigger; tonight's proposal confirms the prediction. This predicted-to-realized continuity validates the forward-signal methodology for tracking threshold events in governance-crisis entities.

ACC dimension is now at 1.0 (floor) — no further downgrade possible on this dimension alone. The INT dimension is also at 1.0. The remaining headroom to the absolute composite floor is 6.3 points, concentrated in AWR (1.5) and SYS (1.5).

Bolivia at the proposed 6.3 is now in the same scoring territory as Iran (2.5), DRC (2.3), and Burkina Faso (6.3). The ACC floor designation means future scoring events must either create a new qualifying harm category or demonstrate such severity in existing categories that the composite floor is warranted.

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Export Control and Regulatory Compliance
sectorAlerts.headline: Anthropic's Fable/Mythos suspension follow-through confirms the regulatory compliance posture established June 12-13; the EU AI Act August 2 GPAI deadline is the sector's next hard enforcement trigger
observations: Anthropic appears as the priority AI-labs assessment entity in two consecutive cycles (June 13-14). The consistent -0.3 to 0.0 composite delta across both cycles confirms that the published 59.1 accurately prices the current Fable/Mythos export-control arc.

The benchmark's methodological ruling from June 13 holds: transparent compliance under government compulsion is net-neutral to mildly positive on the Accountability dimension; the scored event is the entity's conduct posture in response to the directive, not the directive itself.
The EU AI Act August 2 GPAI documentation deadline remains the most consequential unresolved regulatory trigger across the AI-labs index. No assessed lab has published required documentation.
OpenAI's 42-state AG probe (June 12-13) fell outside tonight's scan scope; it will be assessed in a future cycle if OpenAI enters the priority scan rotation.

sectorAlerts.headline: Israel and Russia reinforce at 0.0 while Bolivia descends toward the floor; the Critical-band-to-floor trajectory is the defining countries-index pattern in the June 2026 window
observations: Absolute-floor confirmations tonight: Israel (OCHA June 5: 45 killed May 20-June 3), Russia (June 2 mass strike on Ukraine: 22+ civilians). Both floor states are stable and structurally reinforced.

Near-floor Critical-band entities assessed tonight: Iran (2.5, 775 executions in 2026), DRC (2.3, Ebola ongoing), Burkina Faso (6.3, junta transparency gap). Bolivia's proposed 6.3 would join this near-floor cluster.

The five-country Sahel freshness-ruling cluster (Nigeria, Mali, Niger, Chad, Burkina Faso) represents a structural limitation of the benchmark's lean-season evidence methodology: the most significant humanitarian data is published outside the 14-day window, limiting in-window proposal generation for these entities.

The convergence of monitoring-suppression patterns at the floor level (documented across June 12-13 for Israel/Gaza, Russia/OVD-Info, Sudan) continues: entities at or near the absolute floor are those where independent documentation capacity is most actively constrained.

sectorAlerts.headline: Oracle's June 15 deadline confirmation closes the immediate monitoring window; five rotation confirmations at the Developing baseline
observations: Oracle confirmed at 14.7 (high confidence, zero delta) on the June 15 deadline review. The coercive severance structure (sign-or-forfeit, WARN pay absorbed) is fully priced; no new evidence emerged.

Five Fortune 500 rotation entities (Asbury Automotive, Murphy USA, Five Below, Ross Stores, Global Partners) all confirm at the 25.0 Developing baseline. No in-window material evidence for any of the five.

The Fortune 500 rotation cycle is functioning as intended: baseline confirmations for entities without material in-window conduct events allow attention to remain on priority geopolitical and corporate governance events.
P&G (86.1 → 79.0) and Oracle (20.6 → 14.7) applied on June 14; neither generated new assessment evidence this cycle as they are now at their applied scores.

methodologyNotes.name: PREDICTED-TRIGGER-REALIZED: Bolivia State-of-Exception Confirms Forward-Signal Methodology
methodologyNotes.description: The June 13 digest identified the state-of-exception law as the threshold-crossing trigger for Bolivia's ACC dimension reaching floor. The June 14 assessment confirms the prediction. This is the first documented predicted-trigger-realized sequence in the benchmark's assessment record where the forward signal was explicitly named in a digest before enactment. Methodological implication: the forward-signal framework for tracking threshold events in governance-crisis entities is validated. Future application: when a digest explicitly identifies an imminent legislative or executive act as a threshold-crossing trigger, the next assessment cycle after enactment should be treated as a high-priority event-driven assessment.

methodologyNotes.name: ACC-FLOOR-DESIGNATION: Military Deployment Against Protesters with Repealed Safeguards
methodologyNotes.description: Bolivia's June 9 law establishes that authorization of military deployment against civilian protesters coupled with repeal of the prior 60-day duration cap and deployment restrictions constitutes an ACC-dimension floor event (1.0). The evidence threshold: (1) formal legal authorization of military force against civilians; (2) simultaneous repeal of constitutional safeguards; (3) documented casualties; (4) accountability deflection via evidence-free official justification. This is distinct from the use of police force or a declared state of emergency without military authorization. First application: Bolivia, June 14, 2026. The INT-to-floor corollary: evidence-free official justification accompanying a military deployment authorization moves the INT dimension to floor in the same assessment cycle.

forwardTriggers.trigger: WARN Act layoff deadline — confirmed today with zero new evidence; WARN-avoidance litigation (Washington state class action, Missouri investigation) is the next active trigger
Ebola outbreak July peak window structural review — Bundibugyo strain in Ituri province; documented state obstruction is conversion trigger from natural-disaster-overlay to scored conduct event
EU AI Act GPAI documentation deadline — next hard regulatory enforcement trigger across AI-labs index; no assessed lab has published required documentation
Sahel lean-season monitoring window — Phase 5 IPC famine designation for any Nigerian zone would constitute scorable evidence not currently priced at 21.9
OBBBA Medicaid work requirements take effect — CBO: 10.9-11.8M Americans projected to lose health insurance; realized-harm event requiring formal reassessment

Military-on-civilian casualty escalation beyond the current Ombudsman-documented 10 deaths, or documentation of a second dominant harm category at scale, would be the floor-designation conversion trigger from proposed 6.3

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: Does Bolivia's June 9 state-of-exception law — authorizing military deployment with the 60-day cap and deployment restrictions repealed — constitute sufficient ACC-dimension evidence to support a floor-designated composite score, or does the methodology require a documented second dominant harm category before the absolute floor applies?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Bolivia's ACC is at floor (1.0); composite proposed 6.3 holds above absolute floor on SYS/AWR residuals.
